scope: No target code executed, modified, or other agent results read

observations: /api/save-run accepts unauthenticated client-supplied orgId, bestGene, speedup, fitness, and code; when DSQL is configured it persists the winner as passed with tests 1/1 (app/api/save-run/route.ts:19-69).
/api/seal-bundle is unauthenticated and signs caller-supplied bundle contents with the server HMAC key (app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts:41-80).

The Python sandbox caches one Pyodide instance globally while executing user code through its shared globals; the claimed fresh namespace is not established in code (app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts:23-26,41-77,144-179).
SQL accepts caller-controlled input without a request-size limit and passes timeoutMs into evaluateSQL where it is ignored (app/api/sandbox/sql/route.ts:137-171).

The JavaScript VM injects host-realm constructors such as Math, Array, Object, Promise, and BigInt into the context; escape resistance is asserted by comments but not demonstrated by an isolation boundary (app/api/sandbox/javascript/route.ts:102-118,154-187).
hypotheses: H1: The deployed trust boundaries do not reliably preserve sandbox isolation or provenance of financially/verifiably significant artifacts under hostile requests; the strongest candidates are unauthenticated winner persistence, unauthenticated HMAC signing, shared Pyodide state, and unbounded SQL execution.
deductions: The save-run path can potentially create a claimable gene whose organization, code, speedup, and passing status originate from the request rather than a verified evolution run.
A valid server HMAC on caller-selected content authenticates the server operation, not the truth of the bundle; this reopens the provenance of Level 3 evidence.
The Python singleton and absent per-request namespace reset create a plausible cross-request state-leak and concurrency hypothesis.
The SQL timeout parameter is structurally dead, so resource exhaustion remains a plausible denial-of-service candidate; static evidence does not establish exploitability.
The JavaScript constructor path is downgraded to a reopened isolation hypothesis because no sandbox escape was executed.
evidence: app/api/save-run/route.ts:19-29 has no authentication; lines 38-45 derive orgId and gene identity from request data; lines 60-69 persist client-selected code and mark tests_passed/tests_total as 1/1.
app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts:41-80 performs no authentication or server-side recomputation before passing caller-selected fields to sealEvolutionBundle with PHYLO_HMAC_KEY.
app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts:26 stores a single Pyodide promise; lines 144-225 run interpolated user code and tests on that instance without clearing globals.
app/api/sandbox/sql/route.ts:152-171 forwards timeoutMs as _timeoutMs; lines 210-229 execute the query without using that value for interruption.
app/api/sandbox/javascript/route.ts:106-117 injects host-provided built-ins; lines 159-170 use eval for user code and tests inside the same context.
No dynamic confirmation was performed because target execution was prohibited; static patterns are not treated as confirmed findings.
decision: Prioritize manual remediation review for unauthenticated save-run persistence and server-side bundle signing.
Treat Python namespace reuse and SQL timeout omission as high-priority reopened hypotheses requiring isolated, authorized tests.
Downgrade JavaScript VM escape to an unconfirmed candidate pending a dedicated sandbox escape test.
Do not accept any candidate as confirmed from static patterns alone.

adi.statement: The most economical explanation for the combined findings is incomplete separation between untrusted request data, execution sandboxes, and trusted financial/evidence records.
evidence: Unauthenticated client-controlled winner persistence is present at app/api/save-run/route.ts:19-69.
Unauthenticated HMAC sealing of caller-controlled content is present at app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts:41-80.

adi.statement: If H1 is correct, an attacker may be able to manufacture claimable or apparently server-authenticated artifacts without passing the intended verification workflow.
evidence: save-run writes status passed and tests 1/1 from client-supplied bestGene fields at app/api/save-run/route.ts:60-69.
seal-bundle signs caller-supplied genes and winner at app/api/seal-bundle/route.ts:62-80.

adi.statement: The broader isolation and provenance failure pattern cannot be generalized from this static sample without execution or deployment evidence.
evidence: Python shared-instance behavior is only statically inferred from app/api/sandbox/python/route.ts:23-26 and 144-225.
SQL timeout omission is only statically inferred from app/api/sandbox/sql/route.ts:152-171.
adi.status: abstained with an exact reason: target execution was prohibited by the review scope
